behaviors.description: Cron parser rejects invalid expressions (month 13, day 32, malformed syntax) with descriptive errors.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure is parser/validator logic at a single function/object boundary. REAL-INFRA: none (no OS/network/subprocess). MOCK-HIDE: mocks are not needed; direct parser call exposes failures. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: not applicable. Rejection-gate: higher levels are disqualified by behavior text focusing only on parsing and error messages, with no external infrastructure.
behaviors.test_description: Call `parseCron(expr)` with invalid inputs (`"0 0 1 13 *"`, `"0 0 32 * *"`, malformed tokens). Assert parse fails and error contains field-specific reason (e.g., `month out of range`, `invalid syntax`).

behaviors.description: Next-run-time calculator returns correct timestamps for standard cron expressions, including last-day-of-month and nearest-weekday cases.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: real failure boundary is parser/AST/schedule-calculator handoff for cron semantics (`L`, `W`-style behavior), not just one pure helper. REAL-INFRA: no required OS/network/subprocess. MOCK-HIDE: stubbing parser or schedule object could hide token-interpretation mismatches. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: not applicable. Integration-brake checks: (a) no, (b) no, (c) no, (d) no.
behaviors.test_description: Parse real cron strings and compute next N runs from fixed start instants; assert exact timestamps for normal and edge expressions (end-of-month rollovers, nearest weekday adjustment). Keep all components in-process, no external services.

behaviors.description: Overlap detector flags schedules firing within minimum gap and reports conflicting windows.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure appears when run-time generation from two schedules is combined with overlap/gap logic; module boundary interaction matters. REAL-INFRA: no OS/network/subprocess required. MOCK-HIDE: mocking generated times can miss boundary bugs in schedule expansion + overlap joining. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: not applicable. Rejection-gate: System/Agentic/Workflow disqualified by behavior text (pure scheduling logic, no external model/infrastructure).
behaviors.test_description: Create two real schedule definitions, generate windows for a horizon, run overlap detection with a 5-minute gap, and assert both detection boolean and returned conflict intervals are correct at boundary times (exactly 5 min, 4m59s, multiple collisions).

behaviors.description: Timezone-aware scheduling handles DST spring-forward gaps and fall-back ambiguities for a 2:30 AM job.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure is interaction between scheduler logic and real timezone-rule handling in the date-time library (gap/ambiguous local times). REAL-INFRA: usually no subprocess/network; can run fully in-process with real tz rules. MOCK-HIDE: fake timezone stubs commonly hide DST edge behavior. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: not applicable. Rejection-gate: System considered, but behavior text does not require subprocess/real network/OS primitives beyond in-process time library use.
behaviors.test_description: Using a real IANA zone (e.g., `America/New_York`), compute runs around known DST transition dates; assert policy for nonexistent `02:30` in spring-forward and duplicate `02:30` in fall-back (which instance(s) fire) matches spec.

behaviors.description: If computed next run is in the past, engine applies missed-run policy (skip vs backfill).
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: behavior spans schedule computation + policy evaluation + execution planning state; handoff bugs are the real failure boundary. REAL-INFRA: no required OS/network/subprocess; fake clock/in-memory store is sufficient. MOCK-HIDE: fully mocking either calculator or policy layer can hide incorrect cross-module decisions. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: not applicable. Rejection-gate: higher levels disqualified because behavior text is deterministic policy logic with no external AI or system dependency.
behaviors.test_description: Set last-run/start timestamps so next run is already in the past, then run engine with `skip` and `backfill` policies. Assert resulting queued executions and next scheduled time differ per policy and match expected counts/timestamps.
